body.title: [sprint] Homogenize MCP surface to rack dispatch(to, verb, args)
body.intention: I intend that every rack verb is reached through one dispatch tool by addressee (dispatch(to, verb, args)), so adding a device or reasoner adds zero new tool-name entries.
body.description:
Per-device MCP namespaces re-flatten the whole verb catalog under every device name. The bus already routes by `to_device` (envelope); the tool layer should mirror it. Replace the per-verb tool surface with one `rack` dispatch server - `dispatch(to, verb, args)` + a `manifest` tool for discovery - so the addressee is a parameter, not a namespace.

**Four strata (all reached the same way):** (1) universal baseline - budget/channel/manifest/health; (2) graph-tree MEMORY substrate - memory_*/wg_neighbors/palace_*, fronted by the Librarian (the MCP/memory device); (3) reasoning-layer verbs - traces/tail_heat/habit_list/turn_trace/hot_nodes/attractors - REASONER-CLASS, reached by `to=<reasoner>` (igor=instance #1; NOT igor-namespaced, NOT deleted - routed as-is); (4) genuine role-verbs - librarian_curate/propose_change->Librarian, scraps_*->Scraps. Reuse the already-addressee-based devices/queue/mcp_server.py (send_to/dispatch_ticket) pattern. Server-side validates (to, verb).

**Affected files:** new rack dispatch server module; unseen_university/devices/librarian/tools/__init__.py (SCHEMAS + dispatch); unseen_university/devices/librarian/mcp_server.py; ~/.mcp.json; align with devices/queue/mcp_server.py.
**Design rules:** homogeneity-over-special-case, bus-envelope mirror, igor-graph-tree-reasoner-instance-one (cognition verbs = reasoner-class, addressee-routed, not redesigned), delete-after-successful-merge (the per-verb entries must be DEREGISTERED, not left alongside dispatch). **Inertia: librarian aggregator + mcp_server.py + .mcp.json are design-center/MEDIUM-HIGH - pre-approved by Akien in the design conversation 2026-06-26.**
**Scope boundary:** IN - dispatch+manifest surface, strata mapping, addressee resolution (to=igor -> the running instance, cf. uu_feed.resolve_instance), the single full sweep of LIVE refs -> dispatch, and DEREGISTERING the per-verb tools. OUT - deep cognition/memory-layer redesign (reasoner verbs are ROUTED as-is, behavior unchanged); OUT - what any verb does; OUT - historical append-only store.
**Test plan:** (1) parity - dispatch(to='igor', verb='traces_recent') returns what mcp__igor__traces_recent did; (2) manifest lists the four strata/verbs; (3) REMOVE-BEHIND (machine-verifiable): the per-verb tool entries are no longer individually registered - registered/SCHEMAS tool count <= N (small fixed set: dispatch + manifest + any genuinely-kept). A hollow build that adds dispatch ALONGSIDE the 43 FAILS this; (4) adding a fake device adds zero new tool-name entries; (5) the deferred-tool count drops (the hypothesis meter).

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)
[hard_block] ‚õî NO SQLITE. EVER. POSTGRES OR FLAT-FILE ONLY. See banner at top of this file. ‚Äî applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status ‚Äî applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] No TheIgors imports. UnseenUniversity must be portable without TheIgors present. ‚Äî applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. ‚Äî applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). ‚Äî applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] Canonical dev-process memory ‚Äî one home, no exceptions. Every dev-process artifact ‚Äî ‚Äî applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from ‚Äî applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. ‚Äî applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. ‚Äî applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. ‚Äî applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] `~/.unseen_university/logs/<device>/<stream>/` is the canonical log hierarchy ‚Äî ‚Äî applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven ‚Äî no discriminator. A ticket that ‚Äî applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
‚Ä¶and 39 more (lower-severity or capped) ‚Äî query constraints_get for the full set

**Completion criteria:**
- `dispatch(to, verb, args)` + `manifest` tools exist; round-trip (to='igor', verb='traces_recent') returns parity output.
- REMOVE-BEHIND (machine-verifiable): registered/SCHEMAS per-verb tool count <= N (dispatch + manifest + any genuinely-kept) ‚Äî the 43 per-verb entries are DEREGISTERED, not left alongside dispatch. A hollow build adding dispatch beside the 43 FAILS this.
- Adding a fake device adds zero new tool-name entries.
- `/context` deferred MCP tool-name count drops from ~86 toward the small fixed set (the hypothesis meter).

## Addendum 2026-06-26 ‚Äî addressee is the INSTANCE, never the class
Akien's class/instance model (see memory project-class-vs-instance-addressee). The `to=` in dispatch(to, verb, args) and the manifest's addressee list MUST be INSTANCES, not classes:
- `igor` (class) is NEVER an addressee ‚Äî `Wild1` (instance) is. `Igor-Wild-0001` is a different instance of the same class.
- `granny`‚Üí`GrannyWeatherwax`, `claude`‚Üí`cc.0`/`RESCUE`, `ds`‚Üí`DS.0`/`DS.1`.
- Singletons (`librarian`/TheLibrarian, GrannyWeatherwax) collapse class‚âàinstance by convention, but the `to=` slot is semantically the instance; **class is a metadata facet** of the manifest entry (kind / role / support-team / memory-sharing model), not the address.
- A multi-consciousness instance (Wild1) is ONE addressee ‚Äî which consciousness attends is internal routing, not an external address.
**Design impact on stratum 3 (reasoning-layer verbs reached by `to=<reasoner>`):** `<reasoner>` resolves to a reasoner INSTANCE (Wild1), never the `igor` class.

### Current-state debt (reported by Akien 2026-06-26 ‚Äî fix HERE, not before)
Class-name-as-addressee already exists in the wild and this redesign is its lever:
- The **web** already uses `igor` (the class) as the identifier ‚Äî should become the instance (`Wild1`) once this redesign settles the canonical instance name.
- **granny** shows up as BOTH class and instance across the code (e.g. `from_device="granny"` in tests, `DEVICE_ID="granny"`) ‚Äî inconsistent; normalize to the instance (`GrannyWeatherwax`) as addressee, `granny` as the class facet.
Low priority on its own (Akien held it for this lever); do NOT pre-fix the web label or rename grannys before this redesign decides the instance-naming scheme ‚Äî that would be rework.

## Design resolution 2026-06-26 ‚Äî dispatch = send_to + readfeed + correlation (routing is the RACK's)
Closes the open how-questions. dispatch does NOT route and is NOT a router with a local-call-vs-bus-hop branch ‚Äî the rack (bus/router.py Router, the comms:// resolver) routes, topology-blind. dispatch is a thin MCP<->bus adapter assembled from primitives that already exist:
- **Request:** `send_to(receiver=<instance>, {"kind": verb, **args})` ‚Äî verb rides in the payload as `kind` (existing convention; cf. _feeds_send_to in devices/queue/mcp_server.py). The target's idle_wait loop picks it up, runs the verb, and `send_to`s the result back to the caller.
- **Reply:** the caller awaits the correlated reply on its own feed (readfeed / view_feed) with a timeout.
- **Correlation:** attach a correlation id so the caller pairs reply<->request. The **announce** round-trip (unseen_university/announce/announce_mcp.py = send_to + readfeed + correlation) already implements this ‚Äî REUSE it. Only if it doesn't cover synchronous request/reply with return values do we build that one small correlation+await-with-timeout wrapper. That is the ONLY genuinely-new transport code.
- **Routing/addressing already done by the rack:** comms:// addresses are instance-native (comms://CC.0, comms://igor-wild-0001) and Envelope already carries to_device. dispatch hands to the rack, never decides local-vs-remote.
- **#1 `to=` is the INSTANCE** ‚Äî supersedes the test-plan `to='igor'` literal; read it as `to=<running igor instance>` (resolved via uu_feed.resolve_instance). No class-as-sugar.
- **#2 manifest carries per-verb arg schemas, but LEAN** ‚Äî skills spell out the exact call when a verb is used directly (CC mostly uses bash), so no elaborate autonomous-discovery surface.
- **manifest = the one genuinely-new surface:** {instance, class-facet, kind, verbs:[{name, args_schema}]}, built from the rack's flat-file registry (skeleton/).
- **Cutover order (no broken window):** add dispatch+manifest ALONGSIDE existing tools -> rewrite live call-sites to dispatch -> THEN deregister the 43. The remove-behind criterion is the END state, not the order.
- **N = 2** (dispatch + manifest); nothing 'genuinely kept' top-level.
- **Errors:** structured error returns (not exceptions) for unknown-addressee / unknown-verb / instance-offline / bad-args / reply-timeout.
